{"step_by_step_thinking": "In a cohort study, the risk ratio (RR) is used to measure the strength of the association between a factor and a disease. A risk ratio of 1 indicates that the risk of developing the disease is the same in the exposed and unexposed groups. This means there is no difference in the risk of the disease between the two groups, implying no association between the factor and the disease.", "answer_choice": "A"}
